# Checklist: API Quality
<!-- Generated by /speckit.checklist -->
<!-- Branch: main | Date: 2026-04-06 -->

## Existing Endpoints

| ID | Check | Dimension | FR/US Ref | Status |
|----|-------|-----------|-----------|--------|
| CHK021 | POST /user: No input validation specified beyond "username unique" — max length for username/nickname/password? | [validation] | US1, FR-021 | [ ] |
| CHK022 | POST /group/{id}/transaction: Amount type is u32 in request — what if client sends 0? Negative? Spec doesn't define minimum amount validation. | [validation] | US6, FR-001 | [ ] |
| CHK023 | All endpoints return bare HTTP status codes with no error body. Is this intentional or should error responses include a message field? | [errors] | All US | [ ] |
| CHK024 | POST /group/{id}/member (join): What status code if user is already a member? 200 (idempotent) or 409 (conflict)? Not specified. | [contract] | US5 | [ ] |

## New Endpoints

| ID | Check | Dimension | FR/US Ref | Status |
|----|-------|-----------|-----------|--------|
| CHK025 | PUT /group/{id}/transaction/{txId}: Request shape defined (debtor_ids, amount, description, timestamp?) — but is a partial update supported? Or must all fields be provided? Plan implies full replacement. | [contract] | CLR-003 | [ ] |
| CHK026 | PUT /group/{id}/transaction/{txId}: Response shape — plan says "200 Transaction" but existing create_transaction returns the Group, not the Transaction. Which convention to follow? | [consistency] | CLR-003 | [ ] |
| CHK027 | DELETE /group/{id}: Plan says 403 for non-owner, but existing delete_transaction returns 404 for non-creditor. Should group deletion follow the same 404 pattern for consistency? | [consistency] | CLR-004, FR-003 | [ ] |
| CHK028 | PUT /user: Plan says response is "200 User" — should this return FullUser (with groups) like GET /user, or a simple User? | [contract] | CLR-005 | [ ] |
| CHK029 | PUT /user: Is there input validation on nickname? Min/max length? Empty string allowed? | [validation] | CLR-005 | [ ] |
| CHK030 | PUT /user: Password field is optional — what if empty string "" is sent vs omitted? Both should be treated as "don't change password." | [validation] | CLR-005 | [ ] |
| CHK031 | All 3 new endpoints: openapi.yaml must be updated (T032, T035) — verify request/response schemas match actual implementation. | [contract] | CLR-003, CLR-004, CLR-005 | [ ] |
